title-group.article-title: Role of BRCA1 in DNA Repair Mechanisms

abstract.p: BRCA1 is a key tumor suppressor gene involved in DNA damage response. This study examines the interaction between BRCA1 and TP53 in breast cancer cells.

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: Breast cancer remains a significant health concern. The BRCA1 gene [1] plays a crucial role in maintaining genomic stability through homologous recombination repair.

sec.title: Methods
sec.p: We used CRISPR-Cas9 to generate BRCA1 knockout cell lines. Western blot analysis confirmed loss of BRCA1 protein expression. Co-immunoprecipitation experiments were performed with anti-TP53 antibodies.

sec.title: Results
sec.p: Our analysis revealed that BRCA1 directly interacts with TP53 through the BRCT domain. Treatment with cisplatin induced α-synuclein aggregation in BRCA1-deficient cells.

sec.title: Discussion
sec.p: These findings demonstrate a novel mechanism linking BRCA1 and TP53 in the DNA damage response pathway. The role of ERK2 (MAPK1) in this signaling cascade warrants further investigation.
